episodes.title: Pritzker denies traveling on Epstein’s plane after Bill Clinton’s since-retracted testimony
episodes.description:
Gov. JB Pritzker is clarifying he never flew on Jeffery Epstein's private plane, after former President Bill Clinton brought up Pritzker’s name during a deposition with the House Oversight Committee. 


For video licensing inquiries, contact: licensing@veritone.com

episodes.title: Illinois lawmakers propose bipartisan commission to investigate crimes in Epstein files
episodes.description:
Illinois lawmakers and advocates Monday announced their intentions to form a bipartisan commission to investigate crimes in the Epstein files that happened in the state. 


For video licensing inquiries, contact: licensing@veritone.com

episodes.title: Former UK ambassador Peter Mandelson arrested in probe into Epstein ties
episodes.description:
U.K. police have arrested former British Ambassador to the United States Peter Mandelson in connection to a misconduct probe over his ties with Jeffrey Epstein. 


For video licensing inquiries, contact: licensing@veritone.com

episodes.title: Former Prince Andrew taken into custody by UK police over Epstein ties
episodes.description:
Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor has been arrested over suspected misconduct in public office after revelations in the Epstein files. 


For video licensing inquiries, contact: licensing@veritone.com

episodes.title: Goldman Sachs' general counsel resigns in Epstein files fallout
episodes.description:
Kathryn Ruemmler is stepping down from her role as general counsel of financial giant Goldman Sachs after her name appeared in multiple emails released by the Justice Department in late January and early February as part of its massive trove of Epstein files. 


For video licensing inquiries, contact: licensing@veritone.com

episodes.title: Clintons refuse to testify in Epstein investigation; House GOP threatens contempt
episodes.description:
Bill and Hillary Clinton refused to appear before the House Oversight Committee to testify as part of its investigation into Jeffrey Epstein, setting up a legal confrontation as the Republican-led panel threatens to hold them in contempt of Congress. 


For video licensing inquiries, contact: licensing@veritone.com

episodes.title: Justice Department says it’s uncovered “over a million more documents” from Epstein files
episodes.description:
Democrats in Congress have said they’re planning legal action if the Justice Department does not release the remaining files connected to the investigation of convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. But the DOJ said it has uncovered more than a million additional files they need to go through. 


For video licensing inquiries, contact: licensing@veritone.com

episodes.title: Lawmakers, survivors upset by limited Epstein files release
episodes.description:
The Justice Department continues to face backlash from lawmakers and survivors over the limited and very redacted Epstein files released Friday and over the weekend. 


For video licensing inquiries, contact: licensing@veritone.com

episodes.title: Lawmakers upset with Epstein file release
episodes.description:
CBS News has learned at least 15 documents related to Jeffrey Epstein that were initially released online by the U.S. Department of Justice have disappeared. Cristian Benavides reports. 


For video licensing inquiries, contact: licensing@veritone.com

episodes.title: Trump Administration faces scrutiny over DOJ's handling of Epstein files
episodes.description:
The Justice Department released additional documents in the Jeffrey Epstein case. At the same time, at least 15 newly released Epstein files disappeared from the Justice Department’s website. 


For video licensing inquiries, contact: licensing@veritone.com

episodes.title: Trump admin under scrutiny over DOJ’s handling on releasing Epstein files
episodes.description:
At least 16 files disappeared from the Justice Department's website on Saturday, including a photograph showing President Trump. Hundreds of pages were also fully redacted, upsetting survivors and some lawmakers. 


For video licensing inquiries, contact: licensing@veritone.com
